LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FERNANDO PUGLISI'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT (CCP 425.16). A R G U E D - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DENIED. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS SHOWN THAT THE ANTI-SLAPP STATUTE APPLIES TO THIS CASE PER CCP 425.16((E)(3) AND (4), PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A POSSIBILITY OF PREVAILING ON ITS CLAIMS. AS TO THE FIRST PRONG, ANIMAL WELFARE IS AN ISSUE OF PUBLIC INTEREST AND COMMUNICATIONS ABOUT AN UNFIT KENNEL AFFECTS A LARGE GROUP OF PERSONS OTHER THAN THE PARTIES. AS TO THE SECOND PRONG, PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED EVIDENCE OF MALICE (PARTICULARLY THE STATEMENTS ABOUT THE USE OF A SHOCK COLLAR THAT COULD BE FOUND TO HAVE BEEN MADE IN RECKLESS DISREGARD) THAT COULD DEFEAT THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE AND SATISFY THE SULLIVAN STANDARD. THE STATEMENTS ATTRIBUTED TO THE DEFENDANTS ARE OF VERIFIABLE FACT, NOT OPINION, AND THUS CAN BE THE SUBJECT OF DEFAMATION CLAIMS. COURT OVERRULES ALL EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO BE ALLOWED TO CONDUCT LIMITED DISCOVERY, THE MOTION IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES A LIBEL CLAIM. THEREFORE, DEFENDANT'S DEFENSES THAT IMPLICATE MALICE WILL BE AN ISSUE WHEN ADJUDICATING THE ANTI-SLAPP MOTION. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO DEPOSE THE TWO TREATING VETERINARIANS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike Complaint (Ccp 425.16)
Off Calendar Oct-05-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JEFFREY A. BARUH, ESQ. of Adleson Hess & Kelly, Tel No. 408-341-0234 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOSEPHINE MASON ELLIS, ESQ., Tel No. 510-301-7811 appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FERNANDO PUGLISI'S Special Motion To Strike Complaint (Ccp 425.16) is denied. While defendant has shown that the anti-SLAPP statute applies to this case per CCP 425.16((e)(3) and (4), plaintiff has shown a possibility of prevailing on its claims. As to the first prong, animal welfare is an issue of public interest and communications about an unfit kennel affects a large group of persons other than the parties. As to the second prong, plaintiff has presented evidence of malice (particularly the statements about the use of a shock collar that could be found to have been made in reckless disregard) that could defeat the common interest privilege and satisfy the Sullivan standard. The statements attributed to the defendants are of verifiable fact, not opinion, and thus can be the subject of defamation claims. Court overrules all evidentiary objections. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Allowed To Conduct Limited Discovery
Off Calendar Sep-08-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NICOLE S. ADAM-HESS, ESQ. of Adelson, Hess & Kelly, Tel No. 408-341-0234 appearing telephonically for the Plaintiff;
Atty. JOSEPHINE MASON ELLIS, ESQ. of Kanner & Whiteley, LLC, Tel No. 510-301-7811 appearing for the Defendant Fernando Puglisi.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to Plaintiff's Motion To Be Allowed To Conduct Limited Discovery, the motion is granted in part. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges a libel claim. Therefore, defendant's defenses that implicate malice will be an issue when adjudicating the anti-SLAPP motion. Plaintiff has leave to depose the two treating veterinarians. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
